description: 5-question yes/no gate applied before full scoring. Cheap pre-filter for triaging large idea backlogs. 4-5 yes = full scoring, 2-3 yes = scoring with caution note, 0-1 yes = reject.

questions.question: Is there a named competitor with public revenue evidence?
questions.rationale: No named competitor = pure speculation. This is the single strongest predictor of shippable ideas.
questions.pass_criteria: The idea description or research sources cite a specific product by name with a specific revenue figure (MRR, ARR, total sales).

questions.question: Is the total addressable market larger than $100M globally?
questions.rationale: Solo builders can live in niche markets, but $100M floor filters out truly tiny markets.
questions.pass_criteria: The target market (freelancers, designers, gamers, students, etc.) has more than ~1M global potential users.

questions.question: Does this extend an existing app the user has already shipped?
questions.rationale: Asset leverage cuts cold-start time. v2 of something real ships 3-5x faster than something new.
questions.pass_criteria: The idea explicitly references an existing Replit app (AniRec, weight-game, ThesPlan, pacing-analyzer, etc.) or reuses a large portion of existing code/data.

questions.question: Can v0.1 ship in under 150 hours of effort?
questions.rationale: Part-time operators have ~20h/week; 150h = ~8 weeks to first shippable version. Longer risks stall.
questions.pass_criteria: The research data or honest estimate for v0.1 effort is <150h. Note: v0.1 = shippable with paying users, not 'finished'.

questions.question: Is there a platform-native distribution channel?
questions.rationale: Chrome Web Store, Figma Community, Raycast Store, Apple App Store, etc. provide built-in discovery that beats SEO alone.
questions.pass_criteria: The product could be listed in at least one platform-native store where users already search for tools of its type.

5.label: GO — run full 12-factor scoring, high-confidence candidate

3.label: CAUTION — score but expect mid-range results, verify the missing criteria first

2.label: CAUTION — score only if the two missing criteria have creative workarounds

1.label: REJECT — only 1 of 5 criteria met, don't waste scoring time

0.label: REJECT — no criteria met, this is not a real opportunity
